title: Curmudgeon dispatcher optimization — precompute pre-review state into a slim artifact + slim the rare-path prose out of curmudgeon.md
do_not_implement: Operator authorizes implementation in a separate step. This PROP authoring run did NOT modify monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md, monitor/prompts/reference/*, monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md, and did NOT create any new scripts. Operator reviews PROP and authorizes implementation as a follow-up.
summary: PROP-020 cut Priority 3 (change-detection) cost ~95% by moving the in-LLM fingerprint scan to a precomputed integrity artifact (drift-audit.json). Operator's manual run on 2026-05-05 confirmed PROP-020 is working but observed that the *path to review* is still slow — curmudgeon spends meaningful time post-clone reading priority-queue.json (50KB, 99% history bloat), human-notes.json (39KB, all 9 notes consumed), tracker.json (65KB, 197 points), and walking the conditional logic for Priority 1 (PROP-009 strict/soft fallback against 467 review files), Priority 2 (coverage-already-exists exception), and the major-external-change audit (Step 0c2). Most runs find no work in 1/2 and fall to Priority 3 — but the LLM has read ~150KB of state and applied ~16KB of dispatcher prose to confirm 'nothing pending' before reaching the drift-audit hand-off. This PROP applies the PROP-020 pattern to the rest of the dispatcher: a deterministic Node script precomputes pre-review state into monitor/integrity/curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.json (~3-5KB, indicates which priority has work and pre-resolves the head item for each); curmudgeon's setup phase invokes the script via bash (~100-200ms wall-clock, zero LLM tokens) and reads the slim artifact instead of all the source state files. Combined with a prompt slim that moves rarely-triggered conditional logic (PROP-009 detail, batch-triage gate, coverage-already-exists, major-change audit) into reference files loaded only when triggered, the dispatcher hot path drops from ~16KB of prose + ~150KB of state reads to ~1.5KB of prose + ~5KB of state reads. The constraint is: actual review depth, lenses, citation discipline, severity grading, and priority order (1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5) are unchanged — optimize the path, not the destination. The expanded review lenses (Advocate Mode, Cross-WIN Consistency, Quantitative Verification) and PROP-009 queue_id discipline are out of scope.

in_scope: Curmudgeon Step 0a (V6 map read), 0b (priority queue check + PROP-009 reviewed-already lookup + Step 8a batch triage gate), 0c (human notes + coverage-already-exists exception), 0c2 (major external change workload audit), 0d (priority routing into change-and-holistic.md).

The dispatcher prose in monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md from line 23 (Review Model — Change-Driven + Holistic) through line 175 (Legacy note on priority-new). ~16KB of prose / ~4K tokens at the front of every curmudgeon run.
The state files curmudgeon reads at dispatcher-time: priority-queue.json, human-notes.json, tracker.json (insofar as it's read for holistic_checks rotation pre-Priority 4).
out_of_scope: The actual per-item review procedure (Steps 1-10 in curmudgeon.md, plus Lens A/B/C in curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md). Review depth and quality are non-negotiable.
Drift-audit.json schema / compute-drift-audit.js / PROP-020 work (already landed; this PROP layers above it).
PROP-019 reduced-set fingerprint compare rule + drift-magnitude tiebreak (already landed; orthogonal to dispatcher cost).
PROP-009 queue_id strict/soft fallback semantics. Pre-resolution moves the *lookup* off the LLM, but the rule itself is unchanged.
Tracker.json schema (curmudgeon writes to it post-review; not changing the write path).
Holistic-check selection rules (just including their *status* in the precompute; not changing what counts as 'pending').
explicit_non_goals_per_directive: DO NOT propose changes to the actual review procedure or the lens system.
DO NOT propose changes to the priority-queue schema, human-notes schema, or tracker schema.
DO NOT propose changes to PROP-019 / PROP-020 already-landed work.
DO NOT broaden scope to other agents (decider, analyst). Curmudgeon-only.
DO NOT propose model-tier downgrades for any phase. Stick to Opus.

instrumentation_pass.method: Static measurement of (a) file sizes the LLM reads at dispatcher-time, (b) prose-line counts in the dispatcher region of curmudgeon.md, (c) decision-branch costs (file scans / multi-file lookups embedded in conditional logic). Wall-clock numbers are operator's qualitative observation 2026-05-05 ~21:15Z ('hard to tell when it switched over from mode selection to work') — no instrumented baseline exists yet (recommendation §regression_protection adds one).

priority-queue.json.diagnosis: 98% of the 50KB read is history blob (175 popped items) + schema metadata (description, mode_legal_values, mode_rules, writers, readers). The LLM reads all 50KB to determine 'queue is empty' — a single 'queue.length === 0' check would suffice. At ~12K tokens/file in Opus context, this is the single biggest dispatcher-time bloat source.
priority-queue.json.every_run_cost: Read on every curmudgeon run regardless of whether queue has work.

human-notes.json.diagnosis: All 9 notes are status:consumed; pending serializes to '[]' (2 bytes). LLM reads 39KB to confirm 'no pending notes.' Same shape as priority-queue.json: live state is trivial; archive is huge.
human-notes.json.every_run_cost: Read on every curmudgeon run regardless of whether notes pending.

tracker.json.diagnosis: Pre-PROP-020, tracker.points was used for fingerprint lookup. Post-PROP-020 (drift-audit absorbs that work), tracker is read mainly for holistic_checks rotation in Priority 4 — 9 entries, ~600 bytes worth of state. The other 65KB is reviewed-history data the LLM doesn't need at dispatcher time.
tracker.json.every_run_cost: Read on every curmudgeon run as part of standard setup.

drift-audit.json.diagnosis: Already optimized by PROP-020. Read in Priority 3 hand-off only. Not a target of this PROP.
drift-audit.json.every_run_cost: Read only when Priority 3 reached. Acceptable.

v6-restructure-map.json.diagnosis: Small, read in Step 0a. Marginal cost; keep as-is.

step_0b_PROP-009_strict_fallback.lookup: For each queue item, the LLM scans monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ (467 files, 9.1MB total) for filename matches against target_id, opens matching files to check queue_id field equality, falls back to substring + reviewed_at>pushed_at heuristic if queue_id absent.
step_0b_PROP-009_strict_fallback.cost_when_triggered: Without a script, the LLM either (a) opens every candidate review file (potentially many MB of input tokens) or (b) makes optimistic assumptions and risks pop-mismatch bugs. The current prompt mitigates by saying 'check the latest review file' — still a bash-glob + jq, which is cheap, but the LLM has to know to do that and has to write the bash. Procedure overhead, not data overhead.

step_8a_batch_triage_gate.trigger: queue has multiple un-reviewed items, all looking like minor re-reviews
step_8a_batch_triage_gate.lookup: For each item: read closed-issues.json (1.4MB!), find each related ISS-NNN's closure record, check severity field. Bail if any moderate/major/critical. Also check review history in tracker.json (65KB). Also estimate diff size from queue item's reason text.
step_8a_batch_triage_gate.cost_when_triggered: Without a script, the LLM either (a) reads closed-issues.json (1.4MB → ~350K tokens) — catastrophic, or (b) does targeted jq/grep via bash — cheap, but again the LLM has to know to do that. Currently the prompt assumes (b).
step_8a_batch_triage_gate.diagnosis: Mechanical lookup — script can pre-resolve. AND: this entire 26-line procedure block (~4KB of prose, lines 121-146) is loaded on every run regardless of whether the queue has any items at all. Pure waste in the no-queue-work case, which is most runs.

step_0c_coverage_already_exists_exceptio.lookup: Walk monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ to find reviews that post-date the note, enumerate question-to-review mapping.
step_0c_coverage_already_exists_exceptio.cost_when_triggered: LLM scans review filenames + opens candidates. Cheap with bash, but again the procedure prose (lines 152-157, ~1KB) is loaded on every run.
step_0c_coverage_already_exists_exceptio.diagnosis: Mechanical-ish — could be precomputed. Prose in the no-pending-note case is waste.

step_0c2_major_external_change_workload_.trigger: monitor/changes/ contains unaudited (audited_at:null) files with classification:critical or classification:strategic this week
step_0c2_major_external_change_workload_.lookup: Walk monitor/changes/*.json (87 files), filter by classification + audited_at + recency. Then cross-check against expansion-tracker.json (380KB), analyst/human-notes.json (338KB), open-issues.json (169KB).
step_0c2_major_external_change_workload_.cost_when_triggered: Same bash-glob shape. 87 small change files (~5KB avg). Procedure prose (line 159, ~1.5KB) loaded every run. Currently most recent changes are classification:substantive or :automated — Step 0c2 rarely fires.
step_0c2_major_external_change_workload_.diagnosis: Trigger is rare; precompute can carry boolean + count. Prose can be moved to reference.

prose_byte_breakdown_curmudgeon_md.diagnosis: Pre-review dispatcher prose (lines 23-175) totals ~21KB / ~5K tokens. Of this, ~10KB is conditional logic (PROP-009 detail, batch triage, coverage-already-exists, major-change audit) that fires on a small fraction of runs but gets paged into LLM context every run. The actual review procedure (lines 176-399, ~22KB) is unchanged by this PROP — that's the destination, not the path.

summary_of_findings: Most curmudgeon runs (queue empty + notes consumed + Step 0c2 not triggered) do ~46K tokens of state-file input + ~5K tokens of dispatcher prose just to confirm 'fall through to Priority 3' — only to then read drift-audit.json and pick the head candidate.
98% of priority-queue.json read content is history bloat. 99.99% of human-notes.json read content is consumed-note archive.
The conditional-logic prose for batch triage / coverage exception / major-change audit (~10KB) is loaded on every run despite firing on <10% of runs.
PROP-009 reviewed-already lookup is mechanical and currently spelled out in prose — a script can pre-resolve the un-reviewed head item, eliminating the LLM-side enumeration.

option_A_dispatcher_state_precompute.name: Single pre-resolved dispatcher state artifact (the precompute pattern, applied to Priorities 1/2/3/4)
option_A_dispatcher_state_precompute.what_it_precomputes: A node script reads priority-queue.json (live queue), human-notes.json (pending only), tracker.holistic_checks (pending list), monitor/changes/ (unaudited critical/strategic count), monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json (path + freshness). For Priority 1, it pre-resolves PROP-009 strict/soft-fallback against monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ and emits the un-reviewed head item with its queue_id, queue_pushed_at, target_type, target_id, context_hints, and a `ready_to_review` boolean (false if content-exists guard fails — e.g., proposal item but expansion file missing). For Priority 2, it filters notes by status:pending and runs the coverage-already-exists check for each. For Priority 3, it carries drift-audit.json's path + generated_at + first-non-OBE candidate-id. For Priority 4, it carries the next holistic check ID with status:pending. For Priority 0c2, it carries unaudited-critical-change count.

schema_top_level.freshness_seconds: wall-clock age at curmudgeon read time (curmudgeon should refresh if >60s)
schema_top_level.recommended_priority: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 — the priority curmudgeon should service this run
schema_top_level.recommendation_reason: human-readable why ('queue has 1 un-reviewed item' / 'no work in 1-3, holistic HOL-NARRATIVE pending' / etc.)

priority_1_queue.head_item: queue entry object with queue_id, target_id, target_type, pushed_at, reason, context_hints, content_exists (bool — false if proposal item missing), ready_to_review (bool)
priority_1_queue.batch_eligible_run: array of up to 3 items if Step 8a gate passes (severity:minor on all related issues, no verdict-change, byte budget < 10KB, no fresh-onboard items, anti-staleness >3-cycle guard satisfied) — empty array if gate fails for any reason

priority_2_notes.pending_items: array of {id, target, note_excerpt, created_at, coverage_already_exists_resolved: {covered: bool, mapping: {question_idx: review_filename}}}

priority_3_drift.head_candidate_already_reviewed_since_au: bool (the OBE check pre-evaluated for the head; curmudgeon still validates further candidates lazily if it skips)

stats.queue_resolution_ms: int — time the script spent on Priority 1 resolution

option_A_dispatcher_state_precompute.who_runs_it: Hybrid: integrity runs it daily (BAU baseline), AND curmudgeon's Step 0 setup phase invokes the script via a single bash call (~100-200ms) immediately after the git clone completes. The bash call refreshes the state file before the LLM reads it — guarantees freshness regardless of integrity's last-run timing. Cost of curmudgeon-side bash invocation: ~150-200ms wall-clock, ~0 LLM tokens. The script is idempotent and re-runs in <500ms even on cold cache.
option_A_dispatcher_state_precompute.curmudgeon_prompt_edit: Replace lines 67-175 (Step 0b through Step 0d entry) with: (1) read monitor/integrity/curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.json; (2) check freshness — if generated_at age >5 minutes, run `node monitor/scripts/compute-curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.js` via bash to refresh, re-read; (3) branch on recommended_priority. Each branch becomes a 5-10 line prose block instead of 30-50 lines. PROP-009 strict/soft fallback enforcement remains spelled out in a reference file loaded only when Priority 1 has work.
option_A_dispatcher_state_precompute.failure_mode: If dispatcher-state.json is missing / malformed / older than 24h with bash refresh failed → fall back to in-prompt scan (the existing dispatcher logic, kept in monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-dispatcher-fallback.md). This preserves correctness if the script breaks. Curmudgeon records `dispatcher_state_used: false` and `fallback_reason: '<why>'` in the review JSON's instrumentation field.
option_A_dispatcher_state_precompute.estimated_savings: Dispatcher-time state-file reads drop from ~46K tokens (priority-queue + human-notes + tracker.holistic + change scans) to ~1-2K tokens (single dispatcher-state.json). Conditional-logic prose stays loaded but can be slimmed (see Option B). Wall-clock dispatch phase: estimated 70-90% reduction (LLM does 1 read + 1 branch instead of 4 reads + multi-step conditional walk). Bash precompute overhead is sub-second and adds zero LLM tokens.
side_effects: New file ownership: classify monitor/integrity/curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.json as `git`-owned (writer = integrity script run from git clone; reader = curmudgeon, reads from clone). Same as drift-audit.json under PROP-020.

Coupling between dispatcher-state schema and curmudgeon prompt — if a new pre-review priority is added (e.g., PROP-022 'Priority 6: external review queue'), both the script and the prompt need the field. Mitigation: schema_version field + tinker Mode 3 audit ('dispatcher-state schema matches curmudgeon prompt's expected fields').
Bash invocation in curmudgeon's setup adds a hard dependency on Node + the repo's monitor/scripts/. Already a hard dep (PROP-020 has the same).

Precomputed coverage-already-exists check for notes is heuristic (the script picks the most-recent reviews matching note's target/keywords) — the LLM may want to override. Make the script's mapping advisory; LLM can disagree and write the full review.

option_B_prompt_slim.name: Slim curmudgeon.md by moving rare-path conditional logic into reference files loaded only when triggered
option_B_prompt_slim.what_it_does: Move four conditional blocks out of curmudgeon.md into new reference files in monitor/prompts/reference/. Each is loaded only when the dispatcher-state (or fallback prose) signals the corresponding priority has work.

specific_moves.loaded_when: Priority 1 has work (queue non-empty + un-reviewed item present)
Priority 1 has work AND head item is win-detail-rewrite/section-rewrite/killshot-rewrite (re-review type)

option_B_prompt_slim.curmudgeon_md_after_slim: Drops from 41.5KB → ~28KB. Main dispatcher logic in 23-175 region drops from ~21KB → ~7KB. The slim version says: 'Read dispatcher-state.json. If recommended_priority is 1, read curmudgeon-priority-queue.md and follow its procedure. If 2, read curmudgeon-human-notes.md. If 3, read curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md (existing). If 4, read curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md §Holistic. If 5, spot-check.'
option_B_prompt_slim.estimated_savings: ~13KB / ~3.2K tokens off curmudgeon.md baseline load (every run pays this). When a rare path fires, the reference file is loaded — same total content, better stratification. Net win: in steady-state operation (most runs hit Priority 3 with empty queue), ~3.2K tokens saved per run.
option_B_prompt_slim.failure_mode: If a reference file is missing, curmudgeon falls back to spelled-out behavior in the dispatcher-state JSON's recommendation_reason. Or — more strictly — refuses to proceed and emits an integrity alert. Recommend: refuse + alert. Missing reference files are a code-error, not a runtime concern.
side_effects: Three new reference files. Each must be added to the workspace-sync rules and referenced consistently from curmudgeon.md.

When operator/maintainer edits queue logic, they have to know to look in curmudgeon-priority-queue.md not curmudgeon.md. Mitigation: curmudgeon.md contains a one-line index pointer to each reference file at the top of the dispatcher section.
PROP-018 (sync manifest) interaction: new reference files need manifest classification. See §interaction_with_PROP_018.

option_C_state_file_hygiene.name: Slim the source state files themselves (move history bloat out)
option_C_state_file_hygiene.what_it_does: Move priority-queue.json's history blob into a separate priority-queue-history.json. Same for human-notes.json's consumed entries → human-notes-archive.json. Live state files shrink to <2KB each.
specific_moves: priority-queue.json: keep only mode + queue + next_id + last_updated + schedule_state + writers/readers/mode_legal_values/mode_rules. Move history to priority-queue-history.json (decider reads when popping, but rarely; can be lazy-loaded).
human-notes.json: keep only notes with status:pending. Move consumed notes to human-notes-archive.json (read by holistic 'review consumed-note coverage' check, but rarely).
option_C_state_file_hygiene.estimated_savings: Without Option A: ~88KB → ~3KB on every run (priority-queue + notes). ~22K tokens saved on dispatcher-time input. With Option A: redundant (Option A bypasses the source files entirely except as the script's input).
option_C_state_file_hygiene.failure_mode: Existing consumers (decider's Step E pop, integrity's audits) need to know about the split. Higher coordination cost. Risk: a consumer reads the live file and silently treats history-as-empty. Mitigation: update all known consumers in one PR.
side_effects: Multi-consumer file refactor. decider.md, integrity, possibly analyst all read priority-queue.json. Each has to be updated.
Schema change to a load-bearing file. Higher review-and-rollout cost than Option A or B.
The benefit Option C delivers (slim live state) is 90% subsumed by Option A (bypass via precompute). Mostly redundant in the recommended hybrid.
Could be valuable as a separate cleanup-PROP later, decoupled from dispatcher optimization.
option_C_state_file_hygiene.verdict: Defer. Most of Option C's benefit is delivered by Option A; Option C's cost (multi-consumer schema change) is higher than its marginal benefit on top of A.

option_D_fast_path_early_exit.name: Fast-path early exit when dispatcher-state confirms 'no work in 1, 2, 0c2 — go to Priority 3 with drift-audit hand-off'
option_D_fast_path_early_exit.what_it_does: When dispatcher-state.json's recommended_priority === 3 AND priority_1_queue.has_unreviewed === false AND priority_2_notes.pending_count === 0 AND priority_0c2.needed === false, curmudgeon skips reading the queue/notes/changes state files entirely and proceeds straight to drift-audit.json + the change-and-holistic procedure. The precompute already evaluated those priorities; the LLM trusts the result.
option_D_fast_path_early_exit.relationship_to_A_and_B: This is the natural consequence of A+B — separately-listed because the prompt edit needs to spell out the early-exit explicitly so the LLM doesn't redundantly re-read state files 'just to be sure.' Without a spelled-out early-exit, an Opus LLM may default to defensive-reading and undo half the savings.
option_D_fast_path_early_exit.estimated_savings: Combined with A+B: completes the ~70% dispatcher-phase token reduction. Without the explicit early-exit instruction, savings drop to ~40% (LLM hedges).
option_D_fast_path_early_exit.failure_mode: If dispatcher-state.json is wrong (bug in precompute) and there's actually a queue item or pending note, curmudgeon misses it. Mitigation: tinker Mode 3 audit periodically samples curmudgeon runs and cross-checks dispatcher-state.recommended_priority against ground truth (independent re-read of source files). If discrepancy: integrity alert + script fix.
side_effects: Trades defensive-correctness for speed. Acceptable because (a) the script is deterministic and testable, (b) fallback path catches script breakage via missing/stale file detection, (c) tinker periodic audit catches script logic errors.
The LLM gives up the 'global view' it gets from reading all state files. Counter: it never used the global view for anything — the dispatcher prose already gates each priority on its own state.

option_E_status_quo.name: Do nothing (the other side of the argument)
option_E_status_quo.what_it_means: Operator's observation ('hard to tell when it switched over from mode selection to work') is qualitative; PROP-020 already cut the biggest hot-path cost. The remaining dispatcher-time read cost is ~46K tokens, but Opus handles that comfortably. The marginal wall-clock per run might be 10-20 seconds — not a fortune at q4h cadence.
option_E_status_quo.argument_for: Adds maintenance: a new precompute script + new reference files + new failure modes (stale dispatcher-state, missing reference, sync-manifest classification). At q4h × 6 runs/day × ~20s estimated savings = ~2 minutes/day of LLM time — not obviously worth a structural change.
option_E_status_quo.argument_against: Tokens are the actual cost driver, not wall-clock seconds. ~30-40K input tokens × 6 runs/day × Opus pricing ≈ meaningful daily spend, recurring forever. PROP-020's ~95% Priority-3 reduction had similar 'is this worth it?' framing and shipped successfully. The same logic applies.
option_E_status_quo.minimum_acceptable_threshold: If the recommended hybrid (A+B+D) doesn't deliver ≥40% reduction in dispatcher-phase input tokens (measured: dispatcher-state.json + curmudgeon.md + reference files loaded vs. baseline), it's not worth the maintenance overhead. Recommend operator pin this threshold pre-implementation.

recommendation.selected: Hybrid: Option A (dispatcher-state precompute) + Option B (prompt slim) + Option D (explicit fast-path early exit). Defer Option C (state-file hygiene).
recommendation.rationale: A delivers the biggest token win (~46K → ~2K dispatcher-time state input). B compounds it with a smaller but persistent prose-load reduction (~3.2K tokens/run). D ensures the LLM actually realizes the savings instead of defensively re-reading. C is mostly redundant after A and carries higher coordination cost. The combined hybrid mirrors PROP-020's pattern (precompute + slim prompt) and stacks: A removes state-file reads, B removes prose, D ensures both savings land.
recommendation.phasing: Ship A and B in the same operator-authorized commit. The prompt slim (B) depends on the dispatcher-state contract (A) being available — Option B's reference-file loaded-when-triggered logic needs to know which priority is active, which comes from A's recommended_priority field. Option D is a 2-line addition to curmudgeon.md once A+B land. Roll out as a single PR with a stage-1 fallback rollout: deploy the script, let it run for one curmudgeon cycle while keeping the old prose intact (LLM can read both, prefer dispatcher-state, fall back if missing); then the second commit slims the prose.
recommendation.minimum_acceptable_improvement_threshold: ≥40% reduction in dispatcher-phase input tokens (measured at curmudgeon's first review-procedure step). If post-landing measurement shows <40% reduction, roll back the prompt slim — keeping Option A's precompute (still useful) but not paying B's maintenance cost.
alternatives_rejected_with_reason: Option C alone — too invasive multi-consumer schema change for marginal benefit beyond what A delivers.

Option A alone (no prompt slim) — preserves ~16KB of dispatcher prose that becomes redundant once dispatcher-state pre-resolves the priority. Inefficient but functional; recommend B as a follow-up if A+D meet the 40% threshold.
Option B alone (no precompute) — saves ~3.2K tokens but leaves 46K tokens of state-file reads on the table. Doesn't address the operator's primary complaint.
Real-time queue-watch / inotify on FUSE — over-engineering. Polling via curmudgeon-setup bash refresh is simpler and freshness-equivalent for the q4h cadence.

deliverable_1_new_script.rationale_for_path: monitor/scripts/ already houses the analogous compute-drift-audit.js (PROP-020) and audit-narrative-citations.js. Same convention: deterministic Node utility scripts run from integrity or by an agent's setup phase.
responsibilities: Read monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json — extract live queue (top-level .queue array), filter to un-reviewed items by walking monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ for matching queue_id (PROP-009 strict) or filename-substring + reviewed_at>pushed_at (soft fallback). Output the head un-reviewed item plus optional batch-eligible run.

For Step 8a batch triage gate: for the head item and up to two follow-on items, look up each related_issue in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json (jq-targeted, not full read), check severity. Verify all minor + no verdict-change-recommended in the latest curmudgeon review for that target. Emit batch_eligible_run array with the items that pass.

Read monitor/curmudgeon/human-notes.json — filter to status:pending. For each pending note, run a precomputed coverage-already-exists check: walk monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ for files post-dating the note, regex-match keywords from note.target and note.note text against review file contents/topics. Emit advisory mapping (LLM may override).
Read monitor/changes/*.json — count files where audited_at===null AND classification IN [critical, strategic] AND created_at within last 7 days. If count > 0, set priority_0c2.needed=true.
Read monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json — extract path, generated_at, candidate count, head candidate id. Compute fresh = (now - generated_at) < 168h.
Read monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json — extract holistic_checks list, filter to status:pending. Emit pending_check_ids array + next_check_id (alphabetical first).

Apply priority order: if priority_1.has_unreviewed → recommended_priority=1; else if priority_2.pending>0 → 2; else if priority_0c2.needed → 'audit'; else if priority_3.candidate_count>0 && fresh → 3; else if priority_4.pending_check_ids.length>0 → 4; else 5.
Write monitor/integrity/curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.json with the schema sketched in optimization_options_compared.option_A_dispatcher_state_precompute.schema_top_level.
deliverable_1_new_script.performance_target: <500ms wall-clock on the current corpus. Achievable: queue + notes + tracker + changes/ are all small (<500KB combined). Closed-issues lookup via targeted jq, not full read. Reviews directory walk is bounded by the number of queue items + pending notes (typically <5 total) × ~10ms per file stat + parse.
deliverable_1_new_script.command_line_interface: node monitor/scripts/compute-curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.js [--workspace /path] [--out monitor/integrity/curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.json] [--max-batch-byte-budget 10240] [--obe-cap 3] — defaults match the curmudgeon prompt's existing thresholds.
deliverable_1_new_script.test_coverage: Add a test in test.js (or a sibling test file) that synthesizes: (a) a queue with 1 un-reviewed item + 1 already-reviewed item (PROP-009 strict match), asserts head_item is the un-reviewed one; (b) a pending note with a matching review file (coverage-already-exists triggers), asserts mapping is non-empty; (c) a Step 8a-eligible batch (3 minor patches), asserts batch_eligible_run.length===3; (d) priority resolution logic for each empty/work permutation. Cover at minimum 6 fixtures.
deliverable_1_new_script.skeleton: Approximately 250-350 lines of JavaScript. Skeleton omitted from this PROP — operator can authorize implementation and the script will be drafted in the implementation PR. Pattern matches compute-drift-audit.js's structure: top-level main() function, deterministic JSON output sorted by alphabetical keys, exit-code semantics, --workspace flag for portability.

curmudgeon_md_changes.section_replaced: Lines 67-175 (Step 0b through Step 0d entry). Approximately 21KB of prose.
curmudgeon_md_changes.replaced_with:
Approximately 7KB of new prose:
```
## Per-Run Procedure

### Step 0: Setup
[unchanged — git clone, read v6 map, etc.]

### Step 0a-r: Refresh Dispatcher State

After the clone completes, refresh the precomputed dispatcher state:
```bash
node ${CLONE}/monitor/scripts/compute-curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.js --workspace ${CLONE} 2>&1 | tail -20
```
This runs in <500ms and writes monitor/integrity/curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.json with the resolved priorities.

Then read the file:
```bash
cat ${CLONE}/monitor/integrity/curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.json
```

### Step 0b: Branch on `recommended_priority`

- **recommended_priority: 1** — Priority queue has un-reviewed work. Read monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-priority-queue.md and follow its procedure with `head_item` and (if non-empty) `batch_eligible_run` from the dispatcher-state. PROP-009 queue_id discipline is enforced there.
- **recommended_priority: 2** — Pending human notes. Read monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-human-notes.md. Use the `coverage_already_exists_resolved` advisory mapping but exercise judgment — override if the mapping looks weak.
- **recommended_priority: "audit"** — Major external change workload audit needed. Read monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-major-change-audit.md.
- **recommended_priority: 3** — Change-driven review via drift-audit.json. Read monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md (Priority 3 procedure) — already PROP-020-optimized.
- **recommended_priority: 4** — Holistic review. Same reference file, Priority 4 procedure. Pick `next_check_id` from dispatcher-state.
- **recommended_priority: 5** — Spot-check. Same reference file, Priority 5 procedure.

### Step 0c: Fast-path early exit (Option D)

If recommended_priority is 3, 4, or 5, AND priority_1_queue.has_unreviewed === false, AND priority_2_notes.pending_count === 0, AND priority_0c2.needed === false: skip directly to the corresponding reference-file procedure. Do NOT re-read priority-queue.json, human-notes.json, or monitor/changes/* — the precompute already resolved those priorities.

### Step 0d: Fallback

If curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.json is missing, malformed, or its `generated_at` is more than 24 hours old AND the bash refresh failed (non-zero exit code): read monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-dispatcher-fallback.md and follow the legacy in-prompt scan procedure (the existing logic, preserved verbatim). Record `dispatcher_state_used: false` and `fallback_reason: <why>` in the review JSON's instrumentation field.
```
curmudgeon_md_changes.byte_savings: ~14KB / ~3.5K tokens off curmudgeon.md baseline. Plus the conditional logic moved to reference files is loaded only when triggered (~10KB / ~2.5K tokens worth, paid only on the small fraction of runs that hit those paths).

new_reference_files.content: PROP-009 strict + soft fallback detail (lines 91-118 of current curmudgeon.md), Step 8a batch triage gate (lines 121-148), per-target-type procedures (win-* / section-* / proposal / killshot-* / prediction-batch). Loaded only when dispatcher-state recommends Priority 1.

Coverage-already-exists exception logic (lines 152-158 of current curmudgeon.md), how to mark a note consumed, consolidation-file naming convention. Loaded only when dispatcher-state recommends Priority 2.
Step 0c2 procedure (line 159), gap-analysis methodology, output filename convention. Loaded only when dispatcher-state.priority_0c2.needed === true.
The legacy in-prompt dispatcher scan, preserved verbatim. Loaded only on dispatcher-state miss/stale. Insurance.

structure_integrity_md_changes.trigger: Daily as part of integrity's BAU run + curmudgeon-side bash invocation refreshes JIT.
structure_integrity_md_changes.logic: Identical to §7g (drift-audit) shape: check freshness, refresh if needed, log status. Failure → integrity Major finding.

deliverable_3_who_runs_the_precompute.primary_invocation: Curmudgeon's Step 0a-r setup phase invokes `node monitor/scripts/compute-curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.js` via bash on every run. This guarantees freshness for queue/note arrival between integrity runs.
deliverable_3_who_runs_the_precompute.secondary_invocation: Integrity's daily BAU run also invokes the script as part of §7h (analogous to PROP-020's §7g for drift-audit). This is a backup — if curmudgeon's bash invocation fails, integrity ensures the file is still refreshed at least daily.
deliverable_3_who_runs_the_precompute.argument_for_curmudgeon_side_invocation: Queue freshness is per-run-critical. The decider can push a queue item at any time; if curmudgeon waited for integrity's daily refresh, queue items could be 24h stale at worst (vs. integrity's 9am cron). Bash invocation in curmudgeon's setup is sub-second and adds zero LLM tokens — it's structurally invisible to the LLM. PROP-020 chose integrity-only with 72h gate because drift state changes slowly; queue state can change hourly, so curmudgeon-side refresh is justified.
deliverable_3_who_runs_the_precompute.argument_against_workspace_sync_invocati: workspace-sync runs every 4h — same as curmudgeon's q4h. So a workspace-sync-side precompute is no fresher than an integrity-side precompute for curmudgeon's purposes. Plus workspace-sync's charter is FUSE↔git syncing, not deterministic precomputes. Adding precompute responsibilities to workspace-sync conflates concerns.
deliverable_3_who_runs_the_precompute.argument_against_new_dedicated_agent: Overkill. The script runs in <500ms; dedicating an agent to it is wasted scheduling overhead. A bash-invoked Node script is the right size.

deliverable_4_fallback_story.missing_dispatcher_state: Curmudgeon's Step 0a-r bash invocation runs the script — if file is missing, it gets created. If creation fails (script crash), curmudgeon detects the missing file in Step 0b read, reads curmudgeon-dispatcher-fallback.md, executes the legacy in-prompt scan. Records `fallback_reason: 'compute-curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.js failed: <stderr>'`.
deliverable_4_fallback_story.malformed_json: JSON.parse fails in Step 0b. Same fallback path. fallback_reason: 'dispatcher-state.json malformed'.
deliverable_4_fallback_story.stale_>24h_AND_bash_failed: If the file exists but generated_at is >24h old AND the bash refresh fails (non-zero exit), curmudgeon falls back. The 24h threshold is tighter than drift-audit's 168h because queue freshness is more time-sensitive.
deliverable_4_fallback_story.schema_mismatch: If schema_version doesn't match curmudgeon prompt's expected version, fall back AND emit an integrity alert ('compute-curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.js schema_version=N, prompt expects M').
deliverable_4_fallback_story.script_logic_error: Tinker Mode 3 audit periodically re-evaluates dispatcher state independently and cross-checks against the script's output. If discrepancy, integrity Major finding + script fix. Same audit pattern as PROP-020's deliverable_8.tinker_mode3_audit_addition.

regression_protection.priority_order_invariant: The sequential priority order (1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5) is preserved by the script's recommendation logic — the script applies it deterministically and emits recommended_priority in that order. The curmudgeon prompt defers to the script's recommendation. If the script has a bug that breaks priority order (e.g., recommending 3 when 1 has work), the tinker Mode 3 audit catches it within ~14 days and a script bug is fixed. In the meantime, the LLM has the dispatcher-state's full breakdown (priority_1, priority_2, etc.) and can override if the recommendation contradicts visible state.
regression_protection.review_depth_invariant: Steps 1-10 of the per-item review procedure (curmudgeon.md lines 176-355) are unchanged. The dispatcher edit only replaces lines 23-175 (the dispatcher region). Lens A/B/C in curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md is unchanged. citation discipline, severity grading, JSON validation (Step 7b), tracker update (Step 8) — all unchanged.
regression_protection.PROP_009_queue_id_discipline_invariant: queue_id presence on queue-sourced reviews remains 100%-required. The script pre-resolves which queue item to service; the LLM still copies queue_id and queue_pushed_at into the review JSON verbatim. Step 7a pre-write assertion is preserved in curmudgeon-priority-queue.md.

a_dispatch_phase_input_tokens.metric: Input-token count from session start to first review-procedure step (Step 1: read our current text). Measure via curmudgeon emitting `dispatch_phase_input_tokens` field in review JSON.
a_dispatch_phase_input_tokens.disprove: If reduction is <40%, the prompt slim or the precompute didn't take effect. Diagnose: check curmudgeon's bash invocation logs, confirm dispatcher-state.json is being read, audit what's still in the prompt context.

b_dispatch_phase_wall_clock.metric: Wall-clock from review_start_at - clone_complete_at (new instrumentation field).
b_dispatch_phase_wall_clock.target: ≥50% reduction in dispatch phase wall-clock from baseline. Baseline measurement required pre-implementation (5 instrumented runs, take median).
b_dispatch_phase_wall_clock.disprove: If reduction is <50%, suggest the LLM is doing defensive re-reading despite the precompute. Check Option D fast-path early-exit instruction wording.

c_priority_routing_correctness.metric: For each curmudgeon run post-implementation, log dispatcher-state.recommended_priority and the actual priority serviced. Match should be 100%.
c_priority_routing_correctness.disprove: If <100%, the LLM is overriding the script — investigate why. Either the script has a bug or the prompt is too weak about trusting it.

d_no_missed_queue_items.metric: Compare queue items pushed by decider vs. queue items reviewed by curmudgeon over a 7-day window. Should match (modulo OBE-skip and fallback events).
d_no_missed_queue_items.target: 0 missed un-reviewed queue items at end of any 7-day window.
d_no_missed_queue_items.disprove: If a queue item goes >7 days un-reviewed, the precompute or the prompt edit is broken. PROP-009 queue_id field on reviews makes this auditable.

e_no_missed_pending_notes.metric: All pending human notes get a review or coverage-consolidation file within their target_window (typically 24-72h depending on note urgency).
e_no_missed_pending_notes.disprove: Human-notes.json or the precompute logic missed a pending entry.

f_step_0c2_audit_correctness.metric: If priority_0c2.needed===true in dispatcher-state, curmudgeon writes a REACTIVE-AUDIT-* file within 1 run.
f_step_0c2_audit_correctness.disprove: Major external change audit being skipped despite needed=true means the prompt edit is wrong about the audit branch.

g_fallback_engagement_rate.target: <5% of runs (i.e., dispatcher-state should be available and fresh on >95% of runs).
g_fallback_engagement_rate.disprove: Higher fallback rate means the precompute is unreliable — investigate the bash invocation or the integrity §7h refresh logic.

h_review_quality_invariant.metric: Spot-check 3 post-implementation reviews vs. similar pre-implementation reviews (same target). Compare on: holes_found count, severity distribution, lens_application (advocate_mode + cross_win_consistency + quantitative_verification field presence), kernel_of_truth depth.
h_review_quality_invariant.target: No regression — depth and rigor should be unchanged.
h_review_quality_invariant.disprove: If post-implementation reviews are visibly shallower, either the LLM is conserving budget too aggressively or the prompt slim removed something it shouldn't have. Roll back the slim, keep only the precompute.

regression_protection.rollback_plan: If post-landing measurements show <40% input-token reduction or any review-quality regression: (1) keep the precompute script (it's harmless); (2) revert curmudgeon.md to the pre-slim version; (3) keep the new reference files in place but mark them advisory. The precompute alone delivers most of Option A's value even without the prompt slim. This rollback is reversible by a single commit revert.

interaction_with_PROP_018.summary: PROP-018 (sync-manifest, status: pending-operator-review as of 2026-05-05T19:30Z) replaces build.js OWNERSHIP + workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH/smart_copy enumeration with a single monitor/sync-manifest.json. Any new precompute artifact created by PROP-021 needs classification.
interaction_with_PROP_018.argument: PROP-021's new files (monitor/integrity/curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.json, monitor/scripts/compute-curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.js, monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-priority-queue.md, curmudgeon-human-notes.md, curmudgeon-major-change-audit.md, curmudgeon-dispatcher-fallback.md) all fit existing classification patterns: dispatcher-state.json is `git`-owned (writer = integrity script + curmudgeon-side bash invocation, both run from git clone); the script is `git`-owned source code; the four .md files are git-owned prompt content (consistent with monitor/prompts/*.md being git-owned per CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules). When PROP-018 lands, these classifications get migrated into the manifest just like every other file. Until then, PROP-021 lands with the existing 3-file convention (build.js OWNERSHIP, workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH, CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules) updated in lock-step — same protocol as PROP-020.
interaction_with_PROP_018.if_PROP_018_lands_first: PROP-021's new files get manifest entries in the same PR that lands PROP-021 — no extra coordination needed.
interaction_with_PROP_018.if_PROP_021_lands_first: PROP-018's eventual landing migrates PROP-021's classifications into the manifest. PROP-018 already accounts for this kind of migration (its starting_inputs_from_prop_017 documents the multi-file-edit pattern PROP-021 uses).
interaction_with_PROP_018.no_block: Confirmed: PROP-021 does not block on PROP-018 and PROP-018 does not block on PROP-021. Independent rollout.

dispatcher_phase_input_token_reduction.baseline: ~46K tokens of state-file input + ~5K tokens of dispatcher prose = ~51K tokens dispatcher-time at session start.
dispatcher_phase_input_token_reduction.post_optimization: ~2K tokens dispatcher-state.json + ~1.5K tokens slim curmudgeon.md dispatcher region + (~3K reference file IF a non-Priority-3 path triggers, else 0) = ~3.5-6.5K tokens.
dispatcher_phase_input_token_reduction.estimated_reduction: ~88-93% on dispatcher-phase input tokens for the common Priority-3 path. ~75-85% for runs that trigger Priority 1/2/0c2 (because the reference file gets loaded). Conservative claim: ≥70% across all run types.
dispatcher_phase_input_token_reduction.minimum_acceptable_threshold: ≥40%. Below that, roll back the prompt slim (keep the script).

wall_clock_dispatcher_phase.baseline_estimate: Operator's qualitative observation 2026-05-05 ('hard to tell when it switched over') suggests ~30-90 seconds of dispatcher-time activity. No instrumented baseline yet — recommendation §regression_protection.b adds one.
wall_clock_dispatcher_phase.post_optimization_estimate: ~3-10 seconds (single file read + branch). 70-90% wall-clock reduction is plausible, matching the input-token reduction.
wall_clock_dispatcher_phase.operator_visibility: The 'mode selection → work' transition becomes a single read + a single bash invocation. The LLM emits a one-line status before reading the dispatcher-state ('Reading dispatcher state...') and another before the review ('Beginning Priority 3 review of WIN-050...') — clear handoff signal.

opus_token_spend_daily.baseline_per_run: ~51K input tokens × 6 runs/day = ~306K input tokens/day on dispatcher phase alone. (Rough estimate; varies with prompt caching.)
opus_token_spend_daily.post_optimization_per_run: ~5K × 6 = ~30K input tokens/day on dispatcher phase. ~270K tokens/day saved.
opus_token_spend_daily.context_savings: Frees ~46K tokens of context for the actual review work, which Opus can spend on deeper Lens C quantitative verification or richer cross-WIN consistency analysis. Possible secondary effect: review depth could improve, not just stay flat.

estimated_impact.structural_resilience: Permanent. Future additions to the dispatcher (e.g., a new priority for external review queue, or a refined batch-triage gate) extend the script + add a recommended_priority value, not 50 lines of new prompt prose. The dispatcher prose stays slim; complexity goes into the script where it can be tested.
estimated_impact.review_quality: Unchanged. The actual review depth, lenses, and citation discipline are unaffected — only the picker is replaced. Tinker Mode 3 audit (in regression_protection.h) verifies this empirically.
estimated_impact.maintenance_cost: Modest. One new ~300-line script + four reference files (one of which is a verbatim copy of existing prose for fallback). Same maintenance shape as PROP-020. Tinker Mode 3 quarterly audit ensures the script stays in sync with curmudgeon prompt expectations.

new_failure_modes_introduced: Dispatcher-state.json missing/stale → fallback path. Mitigation: 24h staleness threshold + integrity §7h backup refresh + curmudgeon-side bash refresh. Three layers.

Script logic bug (e.g., misclassifying a queue item as already-reviewed) → silently skipping work. Mitigation: tinker Mode 3 audit cross-checks the script's recommendation against independent re-evaluation. Period: ~14 days. Acceptable because curmudgeon is q4h not real-time.

Reference file desync (curmudgeon.md updates dispatcher contract but reference file is stale) → confusion in Priority 1/2 paths. Mitigation: schema_version check on both sides + integrity sanity-check that all referenced files exist + tinker audit periodically reads each reference file alongside curmudgeon.md and flags drift.

Bash invocation overhead — if the workspace's Node version regressses or `monitor/scripts/` becomes unreadable, the bash invocation fails. Curmudgeon falls back to in-prompt scan. Same failure pattern PROP-020 already handles.

Coverage-already-exists advisory mapping is heuristic — script picks the most-recent reviews matching a note's target/keywords, but the LLM may want to disagree. Mitigation: prompt explicitly says the mapping is advisory and the LLM should override on doubt.
what_does_NOT_change: PROP-009 queue_id strict + soft fallback semantics — preserved verbatim in curmudgeon-priority-queue.md.

Step 8a batch triage gate — preserved verbatim in curmudgeon-priority-queue.md, but the script pre-resolves the gate's outcome so the LLM only follows the gate explicitly when batch_eligible_run is non-empty.
Anti-staleness rule (no batching if last singleton-deep review >3 cycles ago) — script enforces this when emitting batch_eligible_run.
Severity grading (critical/major/moderate/minor), recommended_action enum (no_change/minor_edit/major_rewrite/verdict_change), JSON schema for review files — all unchanged.
Review file naming convention (WIN-{id}.c{cycle}.json, EXP-NNN-proposal.json, HOLISTIC-{check_id}.json) — unchanged.
Tracker update on review write (Step 8) — unchanged.
Alert append on critical/major findings (Step 10) — unchanged.
Citation discipline + V6 translation map — unchanged.
interaction_with_other_proposals: PROP-019 reduced-set fingerprint compare: orthogonal. PROP-021 doesn't touch the fingerprint comparison logic.

PROP-020 drift-audit precompute: PROP-021 builds on it. The dispatcher-state's priority_3_drift block is a thin wrapper around drift-audit.json's existing fields. No changes to PROP-020's compute-drift-audit.js.
PROP-018 sync manifest: see §interaction_with_PROP_018. Independent rollout.
PROP-014 amendments / DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception: this PROP marks DIRECTIVE-20260505-005 as completed using that exception. No expansion to amendment-001 needed.
PROP-009 / 009r / 009r2 queue_id discipline: preserved verbatim. Script enforces it when emitting batch_eligible_run.
operator_workflow_changes: When operator pushes a priority-queue item via direct git commit (the 'escape hatch' path documented in CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules), the next curmudgeon run's bash invocation regenerates dispatcher-state with the new item — visible to curmudgeon within 1 run regardless of whether integrity has refreshed.
When operator adds a pending human note: same — next curmudgeon run picks it up via the bash refresh.
When operator wants to debug: dispatcher-state.json is human-readable and shows the script's full reasoning. No more 'why did curmudgeon skip this?' guesswork.

open_design_questions_addressed.question: Should the precompute artifacts (if any) live in monitor/curmudgeon/ (reader's directory) or monitor/integrity/ (where compute-drift-audit.js's output lives)? Argue consistency with PROP-020's choice.
open_design_questions_addressed.answer: monitor/integrity/curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.json (writer's directory). Same convention as PROP-020 for compute-drift-audit.js → monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json. The writer is integrity (and the script invoked from curmudgeon's setup phase, but the artifact's canonical home is the integrity directory). This keeps all deterministic precomputes in one place — operator and other agents auditing the precompute pipeline have a single directory to inspect. The 'reader's directory for readability' counter-argument is the same as in PROP-020 and answered the same way: curmudgeon already reads from many directories (priority-queue.json, human-notes.json, tracker.json, drift-audit.json, etc.); a single read of monitor/integrity/curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.json is no different. Pure convention.

open_design_questions_addressed.question: If a precompute is added, who runs it? Integrity (slow cadence, may be too slow for queue freshness)? Workspace-sync (hourly, already runs frequently)? Curmudgeon's own setup phase (defeats the purpose if it's still in-LLM)? Or a new dedicated cheap script invoked by curmudgeon's setup?
open_design_questions_addressed.answer: Hybrid: curmudgeon's Step 0a-r setup phase invokes the script via bash on every run (~150-200ms wall-clock, ~0 LLM tokens — bash invocation is structurally invisible to the LLM, so 'still in-LLM' doesn't apply). PLUS integrity's daily BAU run (§7h, analogous to PROP-020's §7g) refreshes as a backup. Why hybrid: queue freshness is per-run-critical (decider pushes can land between integrity runs), but a cron-only refresh in integrity could be 24h stale at worst. The bash invocation in curmudgeon's setup guarantees freshness without LLM cost. The integrity backup is insurance — if curmudgeon-side bash fails, the file is at most 24h old, not 7 days. Workspace-sync is rejected because (a) its q4h cadence is no fresher than integrity-only for curmudgeon's purposes, (b) its charter is FUSE↔git syncing, not deterministic precomputes — adding precompute responsibilities conflates concerns. New dedicated agent rejected because <500ms-script doesn't justify a scheduling artifact.

open_design_questions_addressed.question: Is there value in instrumentation alongside the optimization — e.g., have curmudgeon emit a 'dispatch_phase_seconds' field in its review JSON so we can measure the impact post-landing? Argue.
open_design_questions_addressed.answer: Yes. Add three fields to the review JSON's instrumentation block: `dispatch_phase_seconds` (wall-clock from session start to first review-procedure step), `dispatcher_state_used` (bool — did the precompute work, or did we fall back?), and `dispatcher_state_age_seconds` (freshness at read time). Cost: 3 extra JSON fields per review. Benefit: post-landing measurement is empirical, not speculative — operator can run regression_protection.a/b/g checks against actual data. Without these fields, we'd have to retroactively estimate impact from prompt-cache stats, which is noisier and less actionable. Recommend including these fields in the implementation; this is a 5-minute prompt addition with permanent forward value.

open_design_questions_addressed.question: Does the holistic-review path (Priority 4) have similar dispatcher-side overhead, or is it cheap? If it's cheap, scope this directive to Priorities 1/2/3 only; if it's expensive, address it too.
open_design_questions_addressed.answer: Address Priority 4 too — but the cost is small and the fix is small. Priority 4's dispatcher-side overhead is currently the tracker.holistic_checks read (~600 bytes of meaningful state buried in 65KB of tracker.json). The script's deliverable_1 already enumerates pending holistic check IDs and emits next_check_id, so the LLM doesn't need to read tracker.json just for Priority 4 routing. Inclusion is essentially free — the script reads tracker once and emits two pieces of state (pending_check_ids, next_check_id) rather than the LLM reading 65KB to find 9 entries. Priority 5 (spot-check) is genuinely cheap and orthogonal — it picks a random previously-reviewed item, no state-file walk needed beyond the tracker.points list. The script can emit a `priority_5_spotcheck.eligible_count` field if the LLM wants to confirm there are items to spot-check, but it's not a meaningful cost source. Conclusion: include Priority 4 in the precompute (small additional code, same artifact); leave Priority 5 alone (no cost to address).

open_design_questions_addressed.question: Should the optimization preserve the current sequential priority evaluation (1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5) or could it shift to a parallel-look-then-route pattern (precompute all priorities' state, single LLM read picks the path)? Argue.
open_design_questions_addressed.answer: Shift to parallel-look-then-route in the script; preserve sequential semantics in the recommendation. The script evaluates ALL priorities in one pass (queue + notes + 0c2 + drift-audit + holistic — total ~500ms) and emits `recommended_priority` based on the existing sequential rule (1 first, then 2, etc.). The LLM does ONE read and ONE branch. From the LLM's perspective this is parallel-look (the precompute revealed everything at once) → sequential-route (the LLM follows the script's recommendation, which respects the priority order). Why this is correct: the sequential priority order is a *correctness* invariant (queue items must be serviced before holistic reviews, etc.), and the script enforces that invariant deterministically. Moving the evaluation off the LLM doesn't break the invariant — it strengthens it (deterministic > probabilistic). The LLM could still override if the recommendation looks wrong (e.g., dispatcher-state says Priority 3 but priority_1_queue.has_unreviewed === true — clear contradiction), but this is an integrity escape valve, not a normal pattern. Counter-argument considered: 'parallel-look loses the LLM's holistic judgment.' The dispatcher's job is mechanical priority resolution, not holistic judgment — there's no holistic decision happening at dispatcher-time. The LLM's judgment is for the per-item review work (Steps 1-10), which is unchanged.

the_other_side_arguments.main_gain: Dispatcher-phase input tokens drop ~70-90% per run. Wall-clock dispatcher phase becomes a single read + branch (operator's '21:15Z manual run was hard to tell when mode selection ended' problem disappears). At q4h × 6 runs/day, recurring saving forever.
the_other_side_arguments.main_cost: One new ~300-line script + four reference files + maintenance coupling between dispatcher-state schema and curmudgeon prompt's expectations. Tinker Mode 3 quarterly audit is the maintenance check.
the_other_side_arguments.argument_against_doing_this: PROP-020 already cut the biggest hot-path cost (Priority 3 fingerprint scan ~95% reduction). The remaining dispatcher overhead is real but smaller. Adding a second precompute artifact + a prompt slim is more architectural complexity. If the goal is 'cheap dispatcher, expensive integrity' (PROP-020's framing), arguably PROP-020 already achieved it.
the_other_side_arguments.counter_to_argument_against: The remaining dispatcher overhead is ~46K input tokens × 6 runs/day = real Opus spend, recurring forever. The fix is modular — one script + one prompt slim, both shippable independently with reversible rollback. PROP-020's success increased confidence in the precompute pattern; PROP-021 applies the same pattern to the next-biggest cost. If PROP-021 doesn't deliver ≥40% reduction, roll it back. The risk is bounded.
the_other_side_arguments.argument_against_curmudgeon_side_bash_in: Adding a bash invocation to curmudgeon's setup means every curmudgeon run depends on Node being available + the script being runnable. Failure modes multiply. Counter: PROP-020 already established this dependency (compute-drift-audit.js is invoked by integrity from the same Node environment). The dependency is established; PROP-021 doesn't widen it.
the_other_side_arguments.argument_against_prompt_slim: Splitting curmudgeon.md into 5 files (curmudgeon.md + 4 reference files) makes it harder to read end-to-end. A maintainer reviewing the dispatcher logic now has to open multiple files. Counter: the dispatcher logic is already split (curmudgeon.md + curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md from PROP-020). The split is established; PROP-021 finishes it. Plus the slim curmudgeon.md gets a one-page index pointing to each reference file.
the_other_side_arguments.argument_for_doing_more: If we're going to ship a dispatcher precompute, why not also fold in Option C (state-file hygiene) for permanent slim live state? Counter: Option C is a multi-consumer schema change (decider + integrity also read priority-queue.json) — strictly higher coordination cost than A+B+D. Defer to a later cleanup-PROP after A+B+D land and the precompute pattern is validated end-to-end.
the_other_side_arguments.argument_for_doing_less: Could just do Option A (precompute) without Option B (prompt slim). That keeps the dispatcher prose intact (easier to maintain, single file) and still saves ~46K state-file tokens per run. Counter: Option B saves an additional ~3.2K tokens per run AND removes structural waste (loading conditional logic that fires on <10% of runs). It's a small additional cost relative to A and stacks cleanly. Recommend including both. If post-landing measurement shows B isn't pulling its weight, the rollback (revert curmudgeon.md to pre-slim, keep the precompute) is a single commit.

completion_criteria_check.instrumentation_concrete: Yes — state file sizes, prose byte breakdown, conditional-branch state-read costs, token estimates all quantified in §instrumentation_pass.
completion_criteria_check.options_compared: 5 options with tradeoffs (A precompute, B prompt slim, C state-file hygiene, D fast-path early exit, E status quo).
completion_criteria_check.recommendation_clear: Hybrid A+B+D, defer C, with phasing argument and minimum-acceptable-threshold (≥40%).
completion_criteria_check.fallback_specified: Yes — missing/malformed/stale dispatcher-state.json, schema mismatch, script logic error all have explicit handling. See §implementation_sketch.deliverable_4_fallback_story.
completion_criteria_check.open_questions_addressed: All 5 directive open questions answered with reasoning. See §open_design_questions_addressed.
completion_criteria_check.directive_status_field_to_set: completed (this proposal authoring run satisfies the directive).

why_human_needed: Operator must (1) authorize implementation (the directive is propose-only); (2) confirm the path choice (monitor/integrity/curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.json); (3) confirm the hybrid invocation pattern (curmudgeon-side bash + integrity-side daily); (4) confirm the 24h staleness threshold for fallback; (5) confirm the four reference-file split; (6) pin the minimum-acceptable-improvement threshold (≥40% recommended). This PROP authoring run did NOT modify monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md, monitor/prompts/reference/*, monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md, and did NOT create monitor/scripts/compute-curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.js per the directive's do_not_implement clause.
verification_pattern: test -f monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-021-curmudgeon-dispatcher-optimization.json && grep -q 'verification_pattern' monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-021-curmudgeon-dispatcher-optimization.json && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN
post_implementation_verification_pattern: test -f monitor/integrity/curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.json && test -f monitor/scripts/compute-curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.js && test -f monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-priority-queue.md && test -f monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-human-notes.md && test -f monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-major-change-audit.md && test -f monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-dispatcher-fallback.md && grep -q 'curmudgeon-dispatcher-state.json' monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md && grep -q 'compute-curmudgeon-dispatcher-state' monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md && echo IMPLEMENTED || echo NOT_IMPLEMENTED
cross_references: PROP-020-drift-audit-split.json (template — same precompute-and-hand-off pattern; layered above)
PROP-009 / 009r / 009r2 (queue_id discipline; preserved verbatim in curmudgeon-priority-queue.md after slim)
PROP-014-amendment-001.json (DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception used to mark this directive completed)

monitor/scripts/compute-drift-audit.js (precedent: deterministic Node script in monitor/scripts/)
